title: rhamnus crocea subsp. pirifolia
intro: Rhamnus crocea subsp. pirifolia, also known as the Chinese Frangula or Frangula chinensis, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate bowel movements, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The health benefits of Rhamnus crocea subsp. pirifolia include its potential to treat constipation, diarrhea, and various gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce pain and swelling. In herbal preparations, the roots and bark of the plant are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
